misconfig_patterns: Framework has NO authentication concept — no auth flags, no master key, no basic-auth built in
Operators expose --listen 0.0.0.0 by following the standard public-access recipe
Auth only achievable via an external reverse proxy (nginx + basic-auth, Cloudflare Access, Tailscale)
GET /system_stats exposes full GPU + RAM topology to anyone
POST /prompt accepts arbitrary workflow JSON for execution (compute theft)
GET /history exposes completed-job workflow JSON + prompt text + output filenames
POST /upload/image writes arbitrary files to the input dir
GET /view/<filename> reads any file referenced by past jobs

active_probe.false_positive_check: Response must be JSON with BOTH top-level "system" AND "devices" keys — confirmed marker per 2026-05-04 and 2026-06-08 surveys

deployment_tells: Default port 8188, but 176k of 176k+ ComfyUI hits run on alt ports (cloud GPU rental proxies: RunPod, Vast.ai, Modal, Replicate)
GPU operators expose enterprise-tier hardware on residential ISPs (Comcast, Telecom Italia FTTx, China Telecom, KORNET) with no firewall
Two GH200 480GB hosts observed on Lambda Labs commercial GPU cloud, unauth (operator-side decision)
Custom-node loadout in /object_info fingerprints operator's workflow library
pivot_paths: GET /system_stats → GPU model, VRAM, RAM, OS, ComfyUI version (identity + compute-class)
GET /queue → current + pending job IDs (operator activity)
GET /history → workflow JSON + prompt text + output filenames (product-IP class disclosure)
GET /object_info → installed custom nodes (RCE-class chain target)
POST /prompt → arbitrary workflow execution on operator GPU (compute theft, output writes)
POST /upload/image → arbitrary file write to input dir
GET /view/<filename> → read any past-job output file
vulnerabilities: No-auth-by-design — there is no upstream CVE, the platform never offered auth
sources: [CONFIRMED — NuClide population survey 2026-06-08; 186 live unauth observed]
